_meta.description: Technical, financial, and operational feasibility evaluation for proposed Power Platform solutions.

mcq.q: What is the main purpose of feasibility assessment in a PL-600 engagement?
options: To confirm that stakeholders like the proposed interface
To evaluate whether the proposed solution is realistically achievable from technical, financial, and operational perspectives
To replace requirements gathering
To prove that custom development is always justified
mcq.explanation: Feasibility assessment is about determining whether a proposed solution can be delivered and operated successfully within real constraints such as technology fit, cost, timeline, ownership, and support readiness.
wrongExplanations: Stakeholder preference can matter, but it is not the core purpose of feasibility assessment.
Requirements gathering still matters and usually happens before or alongside feasibility work.
Feasibility assessment often prevents unjustified custom development rather than proving it.

msRef.label: Perform fit gap analysis

mcq.q: Which statement best reflects technical feasibility assessment?
options: Checking only whether users like the proposed screens
Evaluating whether the required integrations, data patterns, security model, and platform capabilities can support the solution
Counting the number of backlog items
Choosing a logo for the app
mcq.explanation: Technical feasibility focuses on whether the solution can work with the available platform capabilities, data flows, integrations, architecture constraints, and required quality attributes.
wrongExplanations: User preference alone does not determine technical feasibility.
Backlog size is not the same as technical feasibility.
Branding is irrelevant.

msRef.label: Architect solutions for Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Power Platform

mcq.q: Which statement best reflects financial feasibility assessment?
options: Determining whether the solution can meet business goals within realistic cost, licensing, delivery, and support expectations
Estimating only developer salaries
Choosing the cheapest possible approach regardless of fit
Ignoring long-term maintenance costs
mcq.explanation: Financial feasibility considers total realistic cost, including delivery, licensing, support, and long-term ownership, while keeping business value and fit in view.
wrongExplanations: Developer salaries are only one cost factor.
Cheapest alone is not a sound architect decision if the solution does not fit.
Long-term maintenance is a major part of feasibility.

msRef.label: Project goals for Dynamics 365 implementation projects

mcq.q: What is the strongest example of operational feasibility concern?
options: The proposed solution needs support ownership, release processes, and monitoring, but none are defined
The app icon has not been chosen
A screen mockup is incomplete
Users asked for a lighter color palette
mcq.explanation: Operational feasibility is about whether the organization can realistically run, support, govern, and evolve the solution after delivery.
wrongExplanations: Icon design is not an operational feasibility issue.
Mockup completeness is not the key operational concern here.
Color preference is irrelevant.

msRef.label: Operational Excellence design principles - Power Platform

mcq.q: A requirement depends on near-real-time integration with a legacy on-premises system whose owner is unknown and whose API limits are unclear. What should the architect conclude first?
options: The solution is definitely infeasible
There is a significant technical and operational feasibility risk that needs validation before committing
The project should ignore the integration
The issue can be solved later during UAT
mcq.explanation: Unknown ownership, unclear limits, and near-real-time expectations are clear feasibility risk signals. The right move is to validate before committing, not to assume success or failure.
wrongExplanations: The scenario signals risk, but not guaranteed infeasibility.
Ignoring the integration would be poor architect practice.
Waiting until UAT is too late.

msRef.label: Overcome integration challenges in Dynamics 365 projects

mcq.q: Why should migration complexity be included in feasibility assessment?
options: Because migration never affects scope
Because data quality, volume, mapping, and business cutover needs can materially affect timeline, cost, and deliverability
Because migration is only a DBA problem
Because it guarantees the need for custom code
mcq.explanation: Migration is often a major driver of effort and risk. Data issues, mapping complexity, and cutover expectations can change whether a proposal is realistic.
wrongExplanations: Migration often affects scope significantly.
Migration is broader than a DBA concern and involves business, architecture, and delivery planning.
Migration does not automatically require custom code.

msRef.label: Migrate and integrate in Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement apps

mcq.q: What is the best architect response when a proposed solution is technically possible but operationally weak because support, ALM, and ownership are immature?
options: Approve it anyway because technical possibility is enough
Flag operational feasibility risk and recommend remediation, phasing, or a different solution direction
Ignore operations because the project team will disband later
Treat the issue as purely cosmetic
mcq.explanation: A technically possible design may still be a poor choice if the organization cannot operate it reliably. Operational readiness is part of real feasibility.
wrongExplanations: Technical possibility alone is not enough.
That would create downstream support and governance problems.
This is not cosmetic at all.

msRef.label: How to use the Power Platform Well-Architected Framework

mcq.q: Which statement best shows good financial feasibility judgment?
options: Choose the lowest upfront cost option only
Compare options using delivery cost, licensing, support effort, business value, and long-term maintainability
Ignore support costs until after go-live
Approve custom build because it sounds strategic
mcq.explanation: Financial feasibility is about total realistic cost and value, not just the cheapest initial implementation path.
wrongExplanations: Upfront cost alone is too narrow.
Support costs are part of total solution feasibility and should not be ignored.
Strategy language alone does not justify cost.

msRef.label: Project goals for Dynamics 365 implementation projects

mcq.q: When is a proof of concept most useful in feasibility assessment?
options: When the team wants better screenshots
When significant uncertainty remains about technical fit, operational viability, or solution direction
Only after development is complete
Only for custom UI work
mcq.explanation: A proof of concept is useful when the project needs evidence to reduce uncertainty about whether a proposed approach is viable.
wrongExplanations: Screenshots are not the main purpose.
That is too late for feasibility validation.
PoCs can validate much more than UI.

msRef.label: Perform fit gap analysis

mcq.q: Why should Power Platform Well-Architected tradeoffs matter during feasibility assessment?
options: Because feasibility is only about whether code can be written
Because a technically workable idea may still be a poor choice if reliability, security, performance, or operations tradeoffs are unacceptable
Because tradeoffs are only relevant after production deployment
Because Well-Architected applies only to Azure infrastructure teams
mcq.explanation: Feasibility includes judging whether the solution is viable across quality dimensions, not just whether it can be built in some narrow sense.
wrongExplanations: That is too narrow for architect-level feasibility.
Tradeoffs matter during design, not just after go-live.
Power Platform Well-Architected applies to workload design more broadly.

msRef.label: Power Platform Well-Architected pillars

tf.q: True or False: A requirement can be functionally desirable but still be judged infeasible because of cost, operational readiness, or dependency risk.
tf.explanation: Feasibility assessment considers more than functional desirability. Cost, support model, timing, dependencies, and readiness all matter.

msRef.label: Perform fit gap analysis

tf.q: True or False: Technical feasibility assessment should include integration and data considerations, not just application screens and workflows.
tf.explanation: A technically feasible solution must consider integrations, data movement, constraints, security, and platform behavior, not just front-end functionality.

msRef.label: Architect solutions for Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Power Platform

tf.q: True or False: Operational feasibility is mainly a post-go-live concern and does not need to influence solution selection.
tf.explanation: Operational feasibility should influence solution direction early because support, monitoring, release governance, and ownership all affect whether the solution is sustainable.

msRef.label: Operational Excellence design principles - Power Platform

tf.q: True or False: Financial feasibility should consider long-term support and maintenance costs, not just initial project spend.
tf.explanation: A solution can look affordable initially but become poor value if support, maintenance, and lifecycle costs are high.

msRef.label: Project goals for Dynamics 365 implementation projects

dragdrop.instruction: Match each concern to the most relevant feasibility category.
items: Unknown API limits and unresolved integration ownership
Licensing and support costs may exceed the expected business value
No release process or support team is in place for the proposed solution
Source data quality is poor and migration timelines are fixed
dragdrop.explanation: Feasibility assessment often spans multiple categories. Integration and migration risks are usually technical, cost/value issues are financial, and support readiness is operational.

msRef.label: Perform fit gap analysis

dragdrop.instruction: Match each architect action to the reason it matters.
items: Run a proof of concept on an uncertain integration path
Compare packaged and custom options using total lifecycle cost
Assess support ownership and ALM readiness
Review quality-pillar tradeoffs using Well-Architected guidance
zones: Test whether the idea is viable beyond bare functionality

correctMapping.Review quality-pillar tradeoffs using We: Test whether the idea is viable beyond bare functionality

dragdrop.explanation: Good feasibility assessment uses different methods for different risk types rather than treating feasibility as a single yes-or-no question.

msRef.label: Power Platform Well-Architected

casestudy.title: Case Study - The Idea Is Attractive, but Is It Actually Feasible?
casestudy.scenario: A company wants a new Power Platform solution for field service operations. The proposed design includes near-real-time updates from an on-premises dispatch system, mobile access for technicians in weak-connectivity areas, automated document generation, and a custom approval flow for a regulated service process. Stakeholders want phase one delivered quickly. Early analysis shows unclear ownership of the dispatch system, uncertain API behavior, a possible AppSource option for document generation, limited operational support maturity, and concern that custom development may push costs beyond the business case.

msRef.label: Architect solutions for Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Power Platform

questions.q: What should the solution architect do first?
options: Approve the full proposed scope because the business wants speed
Assess the proposal across technical, financial, and operational feasibility before committing to solution direction
Ignore the operational concerns because they can be solved later
Choose custom development because it offers maximum control
questions.explanation: The proposal contains multiple uncertainty points, so the architect should assess feasibility across all three dimensions before locking in direction.
wrongExplanations: Speed alone is not enough when key feasibility questions remain open.
Operational concerns can derail an otherwise technically workable solution.
Maximum control does not automatically mean feasible or wise.

msRef.label: Perform fit gap analysis

questions.q: Which issue in the scenario most clearly signals technical feasibility risk?
options: Stakeholders want phase one quickly
Unclear ownership and uncertain API behavior in the on-premises dispatch integration
The company wants automated document generation
Technicians need mobile access
questions.explanation: Unclear ownership and uncertain API behavior are strong technical feasibility risks because they affect whether the required integration can realistically work as expected.
wrongExplanations: This is more of a timeline pressure than a technical signal.
Document generation is a requirement, not itself the clearest risk indicator.
Mobile access matters, but the integration uncertainty is the sharper technical risk in this scenario.

msRef.label: Overcome integration challenges in Dynamics 365 projects

questions.q: What is the strongest operational feasibility concern here?
options: The regulated approval flow exists
Operational support maturity is limited for the proposed solution
The AppSource option might exist
Stakeholders want mobile access
questions.explanation: Limited operational maturity means the organization may struggle to support, govern, monitor, and evolve the solution even if it can be built.
wrongExplanations: A regulated approval flow is a requirement, not the clearest operational feasibility signal.
An AppSource option is not itself an operational weakness.
Mobile access is not the main operational issue described.

msRef.label: Operational Excellence design principles - Power Platform

questions.q: How should the architect treat the possible AppSource document-generation option?
options: Ignore it because only custom development is strategic
Evaluate it against business fit, cost, supportability, and lifecycle impact as part of feasibility assessment
Adopt it immediately without review
Use it only if all custom options fail
questions.explanation: An AppSource option can affect both financial and operational feasibility, so it should be evaluated deliberately rather than ignored or adopted blindly.
wrongExplanations: That is an unjustified bias toward custom build.
Immediate adoption without assessment is poor architect practice.
It should be considered alongside other realistic options, not only as a backup.

msRef.label: Study guide for PL-600: Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect
